Yeah, good point. I think it would be nice to take the opportunity to highlight the difference between a module and a package, but I understand if you consider that to be beyond the scope of the current issue/PR.

> So really there is no duplication

I'm sorry if I gave the impression that there was duplication! I just listed the various "types" of stdlib modules to clarify what it means for a module to be part of the standard library, in terms of where the source code comes from (where it is developed), how it's integrated into Julia, etc. I do think the distinction is worth documenting, although perhaps this could be considered developer documentation; in that case, I wonder if it would make sense to include at least a hint about it, say somewhere around [here](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/devdocs/require/).

It was indeed a sorely lacking piece of docs. > let me know if the section headers look OK It might be worth pointing out that `Core` and `Base` (and `Main`) are described as "standard modules" in the [Base > Essentials](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/base/base/#Standard-Modules) page of the docs. I wonder if it would be better to reuse that nomenclature in the newly added section header. Additionally, allow me to share notes I collected for myself a while ago when I was looking for precisely such an overview. There's no guarantee of correctness, but hopefully they can be useful to your PR:
- Some of julia's functionality comes from the [standard modules](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/base/base/#Standard-Modules) `Main`, `Core` and `Base` (at the [base/](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/tree/master/base) directory of the julia repo), as well as various _unpackaged_ [submodules](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/base/base/#Base-Submodules). - Other parts of julia's functionality come from a Standard Library of _packaged_ modules. - Of these, some are developed directly within the stdlib directory of the julia repo; these are the subdirectories under the [stdlib/](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/tree/master/stdlib) directory of the main julia repository, and are [listed in the `STDLIBS` variable](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/v1.9.1/stdlib/Makefile#L44-L48) in `stdlib/Makefile`. - There are also various `*_jll` directories, which are appended to the `STDLIBS` variable but appear to not be actual modules. They seem to be wrappers for external, non-julia libraries like libuv, MPFR, curl, zlib, 7zip, LLVM, etc. - Full list, as of v1.9: - Artifacts - Base64 - CRC32c - Dates - Distributed - FileWatching - Future - InteractiveUtils - LazyArtifacts - LibGit2 - Libdl - LinearAlgebra - Logging - Markdown - Mmap - Printf - Profile - REPL - Random - Serialization - SharedArrays - Sockets - TOML - Test - UUIDs - Unicode - Others live in separate repositories outside of the julia repo, and typically even outside the JuliaLang org; they correspond to the `*.version` files under `stdlib/`, and are [listed in the `STDLIBS_EXT` variable](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/v1.9.1/stdlib/Makefile#L50) in `stdlib/Makefile`. Several of these are (though ideally all of them should be) identified in their readme as part of the Julia stdlib. - Those under JuliaLang org (found with [this search](https://grep.app/search?q=JuliaLang/%5B%5EEj%5D®exp=true&case=true&filter[repo][0]=JuliaLang/julia&filter[path][0]=stdlib/), which is tweaked to exclude JuliaLang/Example.jl and JuliaLang/julia), as of v1.9, are: - [JuliaLang/Pkg.jl](https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl) - [JuliaLang/Downloads.jl](https://github.com/JuliaLang/Downloads.jl) - [JuliaLang/NetworkOptions.jl](https://github.com/JuliaLang/NetworkOptions.jl) - (Not sure why these are kept here instead of in the relevant orgs...) - Those under other organizations (found with [this search](https://grep.app/search?q=_GIT_URL%20%3A%3D%20https%3A//github.com/Julia%5B%5EL%5D®exp=true&case=true&filter[repo][0]=JuliaLang/julia&filter[path][0]=stdlib/), which is tweaked to exclude the JuliaLang org), as of v1.9, are: - [JuliaWeb/LibCURL.jl](https://github.com/JuliaWeb/LibCURL.jl) - [JuliaStats/Statistics.jl](https://github.com/JuliaStats/Statistics.jl) - [JuliaSparse/SparseArrays.jl](https://github.com/JuliaSparse/SparseArrays.jl) - [JuliaData/DelimitedFiles.jl](https://github.com/JuliaData/DelimitedFiles.jl) - [JuliaIO/Tar.jl](https://github.com/JuliaIO/Tar.jl) - [JuliaSparse/SuiteSparse.jl](https://github.com/JuliaSparse/SuiteSparse.jl) - [JuliaCrypto/SHA.jl](https://github.com/JuliaCrypto/SHA.jl) - [JuliaIO/ArgTools.jl](https://github.com/JuliaIO/ArgTools.jl) - [JuliaData/DelimitedFiles.jl](https://github.com/JuliaData/DelimitedFiles.jl) in particular, is [singled out](https://julialang.org/blog/2023/04/julia-1.9-highlights/#delimitedfiles_first_stdlib_to_be_upgradable) in the 1.9 highlights blog post as the first stdlib to be upgradable.

request","color":"88c429","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":9,"created_at":"2018-08-11T09:51:31Z","updated_at":"2022-05-04T13:16:04Z","closed_at":"2022-05-04T11:30:54Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hello,

When doing `Pkg.add("NameOfPackage")`, if `NameOfPackage` is not found an error message such as
```
ERROR: The following package names could not be resolved:
 * NameOfPackage (not found in project, manifest or registry)
Please specify by known `name=uuid`.
```
is shown.

If a package name is not found, maybe `Pkg` could try to "help" users by listing name of some packages whom name is quite near to what user is looking for.

Computing string similarity using for example [dice coefficient](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/S%C3%B8rensen%E2%80%93Dice_coefficient) (see [various implementations](https://en.wikibooks.org/wiki/Algorithm_Implementation/Strings/Dice%27s_coefficient)) between user provided package name and name of each registered package could help.

Maybe comparison should be done after upper casing (or lower casing) both.
A threshold could probably be set.
Sorting by descending coefficient will be required, taking only (for example) five nearest names (however displaying them with correct case)
